Empires & Puzzles Ebba Review: The Fiend-Dependent Flail Master
Ebba promises massive chain damage if you can flood the board with Fiends, but does her Slow speed hold her back in today's fast-paced meta? We break down her mechanics, stats, and optimal pairings.
Ebba Review: The Fiend Hunter's Big Swing
Ebba: The Fiend-Fueled Wrecking Ball of the Plains

Ebba arrived in Empires & Puzzles during the expansion of the "Monster Hunter" (now Plains Hunter) family, bringing a mechanic that players immediately recognized: she is essentially the "Fiend version" of Mr. Pengi. A 5-star Fire Legendary hero, Ebba carries a massive flail and a kit that screams "setup required." Unlike the independent snipers of the world, Ebba is a team player—or perhaps more accurately, a team

player. She relies entirely on her allies to set the stage with Fiends so she can unleash her full chaotic potential. If you like Rube Goldberg machines that result in total enemy annihilation, she might be your girl.

Ebba sports a rugged, pragmatic look suitable for someone hauling a flail the size of a small boulder. Her "Hunter Mercenary" design fits perfectly with the Plains Hunter aesthetic: practical armor, a determined grin, and a weapon that looks like it requires a permit just to carry. The visual weight of that flail really sells the "Slow" mana speed; you can practically feel the wind-up time just looking at the card. It is a solid, grounded design that says, "I am going to hit you with this very heavy object, and it is going to hurt."

When Ebba was released, her stats were quite respectable. However, in the current landscape of 2026, a base Power of 887 is starting to look a bit quaint. Her attack stat of 848 is decent but no longer elite, and her defensive stats (856 Defense, 1617 Health) are average at best compared to the 1000+ defense and 2000+ health behemoths released recently. She isn't made of glass, but she certainly isn't a modern tank. You will likely need to limit break her to keep her survivable enough to fire that Slow special skill.
This skill is the definition of "high risk, high reward."
585% damage to a single target is a massive hit. Even at slow speed, that is going to leave a dent.

The additional hits are conditional. She hits random enemies for each Fiend currently owned by the enemy team. If the enemy team is covered in Fiends, she goes berserk, chaining hits like a pinball. If there are no Fiends, she is just a Slow sniper.
This is arguably the most valuable part of her kit. Blocking
ailments) and applying a massive -54% Attack down is game-changing. It effectively neuters the enemy team's ability to retaliate or protect themselves for 4 turns.

Slow speed requires 12 tiles to charge. With a level 29 mana troop or a combination of mana node (Monk) and bard/family bonuses, you can shave this down to 11 or even 10 tiles, but she will always lag behind the Average and Fast heroes.

If the enemy has a full house of 15 Fiends (3 per hero), she unleashes 15 additional hits. The damage reduces per hit, but the cumulative total is astronomical (potentially over 2500% total distributed damage).
In a typical match with one or two fiend summoners, you might see 3-5 extra hits. That's still significant area damage.
-54% Attack is one of the steepest attack drops in the game. It turns enemy snipers into wet noodles.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Plains Hunter family is all about ramping up damage the longer the fight goes on, which suits Ebba's slow speed perfectly.
The attack boost for the family grows over time. Since Ebba is slow, she is likely to fire later in the match when this bonus has had time to stack up, making her hit even harder.
A 10% chance to reduce incoming special damage to 1 is a nice survival tool, though unreliable. It's the "Black Knight" effect on a passive.
This applies a DoT that increases if refreshed. It adds a bit of chip damage, but on a Slow hero, you are unlikely to stack this high enough to be a primary kill condition.
Regen fits her well. As a Slow hero, she needs to stay alive long enough to fire. Recovering health at the start of the battle helps mitigate early tile damage or fast sniper hits.
Ebba occupies a strange place in history. She was designed as a counterpart to the dominant Mr. Pengi, but the "Fiend meta" never quite reached the ubiquity of the "Minion meta."
Upon release, she was seen as a devastating Rush Attack hero and a fun toy for players with deep rosters of Fiend summoners (like Elizabeth or Senan).

Her stats have fallen behind significantly. The damage percentages are still high enough to be lethal, but her survivability is a concern against modern heavy hitters who can delete her before she gathers 12 tiles.
hero. She is not viable for general defense or offense due to her speed and setup requirements. However, in
wars and tournaments, she remains a terrifying force if paired correctly.
As a Monk, Ebba can withstand status ailments, which is crucial for a Slow hero—getting silenced or mana-slowed is a death sentence for her.
Keeps her alive. Her damage percentages are already so high that she hits hard even without attack nodes. The priority is ensuring she lives to cast.

The -54% Attack and Status Block can completely shut down an enemy team.

Resistance to ailments helps ensure she fires.
12 tiles is an eternity in modern E&P.
Without Fiends on the enemy, she is just a mediocre sniper with good debuffs. She cannot create her own setup.
Putting Fiends on enemies is harder than putting Minions on yourself (which Pengi benefits from). Enemies can cleanse or heal/overheal to remove Fiends before Ebba fires.
This is her home. At Very Fast speed, she can fire repeatedly, keeping the enemy team silenced and weak.
Useful for boss waves if you bring a Fiend summoner.
For many players without the specific synergy heroes, she has become a prime candidate for trading in.
Ebba is not a "plug and play" hero. You build the team
her, or you leave her on the bench.
You need heroes who summon fiends reliably.

who specifically destroy Fiends or replace them with minions are hard counters to Ebba. If they wipe the Fiends before she fires, she loses her chain.
A Taunt hero can absorb her initial hit, preventing the chain from starting if the Taunt hero doesn't have enough Fiends (or if the mechanics interact poorly).

If you pulled Ebba early, she can be a strong "boss killer" for map stages because of her high single-target damage and attack down. However, don't prioritize her over Average or Fast speed heroes for your main raid team. She is too inconsistent for early-game PvP.

Ebba is likely a luxury toy for you. If you play competitive Rush Wars and have a strong Fiend team (e.g., you have 2-3 strong Fiend summoners leveled), she is worth keeping for that specific war rule. Otherwise, if she is sitting at 1/1, she is probably safe to use in the Soul Exchange for a more versatile modern hero.
The community reaction to Ebba was mixed from day one.
Players immediately dubbed her the "Red Pengi" but noted the flaw:
... Ebba has strong secondary effects, so more important to hit all."
Many lamented her speed. One user noted:
"Got her from coins. Not sure it was worth it honestly. She doesn’t have much synergy with my most used heroes."
"Ran with her the last rush war and she was devastating... the enemy melted."

Ebba is a hero who lives and dies by the company she keeps. In a vacuum, she is a relic of a slower meta. But in a carefully crafted team of Fiend-summoning nightmares, she is the executioner who swings the gavel. She demands patience, specific teammates, and a lot of tiles, but when the stars align, watching her flail bounce around the entire enemy team is one of the most satisfying sights in the game.
Keep for Rush Attack if you have Fiend summoners; otherwise, she is likely destined for the bench or the exchange.

Is Ebba worth your mats in 2026? A deep dive into the Plains Hunter hero who mimics Mr. Pengi's mechanics but with Fiends. We analyze her slow mana, massive damage potential, and specific synergy requirements.
